evidence_quotes.quote: Games are beloved by Millions of people around the European Union alone and the fact so many of them are destroyed every day is just soul crushing.
It is our right to own the things we have purchased.
we deserve to keep our games, not have them taken away by greedy corporations that just care about the bottom line.

paragraphs.text: I see the Digital Fairness Act as very helpful already, however it to me this Act is missing any consumer protection for purchases, specifically software. Games are beloved by Millions of people around the European Union alone and the fact so many of them are destroyed every day is just soul crushing. I own a very known game, The Crew, I loved playing it as much as many other people. However that was until Ubisoft, the developer of said game decided they would kill the game, all its servers and the entire playerbase. All of it gone in an instant as I watched the disconnected screen, knowing it was the last time I ever played The Crew. This and many other experiences of many people around the EU and around the globe have broken their hearts, as they cannot play their favorite games. This is why I ask if there could be more consumer protection against videogames and software as a whole. Add to that the fact that the European Citizen Initiative called Stop Destroying Videogames gathered over 1.4 Million Signatures, it means we care what we pay for, and we deserve to keep our games, not have them taken away by greedy corporations that just care about the bottom line. From my and many other gamers hearts all around the globe I hope you think of this idea and I think I can speak for many of them, while also speaking as myself that this change is very much needed for consumers. It is our right to own the things we have purchased. This has gone on for long enough, and we have lost many amazing games, that we shall sadly never play again. Thank you for hearing out my proposal, and I wish you all a fine day.
